era: 2014-present. Hard Rock. E=7. Capitol Hill, Seattle. Republic Records. All-female band.

theory_nodes.concept: All-female Seattle hard rock band: the engine's first all-female lineup. In the tradition of Heart (Ann and Nancy Wilson), but fully all-female. Statement about who makes hard rock and on what terms. The music requires no qualification.
theory_nodes.description: All-female hard rock in the Seattle tradition.

theory_nodes.concept: E=7 post-corridor: degree 248 follows the 245-247 maximum run (E=8/7/8). The tradition continues at high-but-not-maximum energy. Hard rock persists in contemporary Capitol Hill even as the neighborhood's economic character shifts.
theory_nodes.description: E=7 as continuation after the engine's maximum corridor.

theory_nodes.concept: Capitol Hill in the contemporary era: gentrified economically but culturally intact. Queer-positive, theatrically bold, politically engaged. CHOP/CHAZ 2020 as continued site of activism. Thunderpussy as Capitol Hill of 2014-present, not 1990s peak.
theory_nodes.description: Capitol Hill's contemporary character vs. historical peak.

cross_references.relationship: E=8. The grunge maximum immediately prior. E=7 Thunderpussy follows E=8 AIC. The hard rock tradition continues.
E=8. The psychedelic maximum three degrees prior. The 245-248 arc from maximum to contemporary hard rock.

sps_pair.pairing_note: E=7 + E=4. Hoh Rainforest: complex patter-drip percussion. Thunderpussy's structured, human-made percussion (Dunphy's drums) pairs with rain's natural percussion. Both are percussion-forward; one is the most controlled form of human organization, the other is environmental chance.

milestones: FIRST ALL-FEMALE SEATTLE BAND IN ENGINE
capitol_hill_character: Queer-positive, glam-inflected, theatrically bold, LGBTQ+ community embedded
